2605.22785.title: Evaluating Commercial AI Chatbots as News Intermediaries
2605.22785.paper_summary: AI chatbots are rapidly shaping how people encounter the news, yet no prior study has systematically measured how accurately these systems, with their proprietary search integrations and retrieval-synthesis pipelines, handle emerging facts across languages and regions. We present a 14-day (February 9-22, 2026) evaluation of six AI chatbots (Gemini 3 Flash and Pro, Grok 4, Claude 4.5 Sonnet, GPT-5 and GPT-4o mini) on 2,100 factual questions derived from same-day BBC News reporting across six regional services (US & Canada, Arabic, Afrique, Hindi, Russian, Turkish). The best systems achieve over 90% multiple-choice accuracy on questions about events reported hours earlier. The same systems, however, lose 11-13% under free-response evaluation, and 16-17% across the cohort. We further characterize three failure patterns. First, every model achieves its lowest accuracy on Hindi (79% vs. 89-91% elsewhere) and citations indicate an Anglophone retrieval bias (e.g., models answering Hindi queries cite English Wikipedia more than any Hindi outlet). Second, retrieval, not reasoning, failures drive over 70% of all errors. When models retrieve a correct source, they often extract the correct answer; the problem is to land on the right source in the first place. Third, models achieving 88-96% accuracy on well-formed questions drop to 19-70% when questions contain subtle false premises, with the most vulnerable model accepting fabricated facts 64% of the time. We also identify a detection-accuracy paradox: the best false-premise detector ranks second in adversarial accuracy (abstention rate), while a weaker detector ranks first, showing that premise detection and answer recovery are partially independent capabilities. Overall, these suggest that high accuracy can mask systematic regional inequity, near-total dependence on retrieval infrastructure, and vulnerability to imperfect queries real users pose.
2605.22785.paper_summary_zh: AI 聊天機器人正在迅速改變人們接觸新聞的方式，但之前沒有研究系統性地測量這些系統（擁有其專有的搜索整合和檢索-合成管道）在不同語言和地區處理新興事實的準確性。我們對六個 AI 聊天機器人（Gemini 3 Flash 和 Pro、Grok 4、Claude 4.5 Sonnet、GPT-5 和 GPT-4o mini）進行了為期 14 天（2026 年 2 月 9 日至 22 日）的評估，這些問題來自於六個地區服務（美國和加拿大、阿拉伯語、非洲語、印地語、俄語、土耳其語）當天的 BBC News 報導，共 2,100 個事實問題。最好的系統在關於幾小時前報導的事件的多選題上達到超過 90% 的準確率。然而，這些系統在自由回答評估中失去 11-13% 的準確率，而在整體評估中則下降 16-17%。我們進一步描述了三種失敗模式。首先，每個模型在印地語上的準確率最低（79% 對比其他語言的 89-91%），引用顯示出英語檢索的偏見（例如，模型回答印地語查詢時引用英語維基百科的次數多於任何印地語媒體）。第二，檢索失敗而非推理失敗驅動了超過 70% 的所有錯誤。當模型檢索到正確來源時，它們通常能提取出正確的答案；問題在於首先能否找到正確的來源。第三，對於結構良好的問題，模型的準確率達到 88-96%，但當問題包含微妙的錯誤前提時，準確率下降至 19-70%，其中最脆弱的模型在 64% 的情況下接受虛構事實。我們還識別出一個檢測準確性悖論：最佳的錯誤前提檢測器在對抗準確性（棄權率）中排名第二，而一個較弱的檢測器排名第一，顯示出前提檢測和答案恢復是部分獨立的能力。總體而言，這些結果表明，高準確性可能掩蓋系統性的地區不平等、對檢索基礎設施的幾乎完全依賴，以及對真實用戶提出的不完美查詢的脆弱性。
2605.22785.author: Mirac Suzgun et.al.
2605.22785.authors: Mirac Suzgun, Emily Shen, Federico Bianchi, Alexander Spangher, Thomas Icard, Daniel E. Ho, Dan Jurafsky, James Zou
